untranslatable_reason: Pattern detection requires identifying two valleys at approximately the same price level and a confirmation line. These are chart pattern features that cannot be expressed using only the allowed price and technical indicators. Valley detection requires local minima identification and relative price level comparison which is not supported by the indicator set. The 'confirmation line' is a subjective pattern element without a corresponding technical indicator.
translation_notes: Attempted to approximate entry as close crossing above a pivot level (representative of the confirmation line concept), but this is a significant simplification. The core double-bottom pattern recognition—identifying two distinct valleys within ~29 days with ~17% rise between them—requires pattern recognition logic not available in the allowed indicators. A proper implementation would need candlestick pattern detection or custom valley-finding algorithms.
